history.onset: 4 hours ago while sitting and watching TV.
history.familyHistory: Father had a stroke at 68; Mother has high blood pressure.

socialHistory.livingSituation: Lives with husband in a single-story house.

history.rhythmDescription: Heart feels like it is 'fluttering' or 'jumping around' with no regular beat.

findings.general: Alert and oriented, but looks slightly pale and anxious.
findings.peripheral: No ankle swelling (edema). Calves are soft and non-tender (no DVT).
findings.respiratory: Lungs are clear; no crackles or wheezing.
findings.cardiovascular: Apex beat is irregularly irregular. Notable pulse deficit (radial pulse is slower than apical heart rate). JVP is normal.

investigations.ecg: Narrow complex tachycardia, absent P-waves, and irregularly irregular R-R intervals.

labs.fullBloodCount: Normal hemoglobin and white cell count.

managementKeyPoints: Confirm Irregularly Irregular pulse on exam
Correctly interpret ECG as Atrial Fibrillation
Assess stroke risk using CHA2DS2-VASc (Score: 3 - Female, HTN, Age 65 approach)
Propose Rate Control (e.g., Beta-blocker like Metoprolol or Bisoprolol)
Initiate Anticoagulation (e.g., Apixaban or Warfarin) for stroke prevention
